evidence: We continue to invest and augment our cybersecurity program
reach consumers in efficient and effective ways leveraging data and analytics (including artificial intelligence and machine learning)
fail to attract and develop personnel with key emerging capabilities...including skills in the areas of advanced technology, artificial intelligence, machine learning

technological advancements (including advancements such as artificial intelligence, machine learning and augmented reality) which are increasingly important for understanding evolving consumer preferences
increasing role of digital tools (including artificial intelligence and machine learning) and commerce for consumers
the use of artificial intelligence and machine learning in our operations may elevate the risk of third-party infringement claims
the rapid evolution and increased adoption of artificial intelligence may intensify our cybersecurity risks and create new vectors of exposure

as the legal and regulatory environment for artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies continues to evolve, our compliance obligations may significantly increase our costs or limit the extent to which we are able to use these technologies

the use of artificial intelligence and machine learning in our operations may elevate the risk of third-party infringement claims, especially those related to our alleged unauthorized use of third-party tools, technology or content

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI-specific spend, headcount, or named vendor/infrastructure partnerships disclosed in excerpts; only generic references to investing in cybersecurity and leveraging AI/ML tools.
ai_narrative_concreteness: All AI references are aspirational or risk-framed; no named AI initiatives, deployed use cases, or quantified outcomes appear in the excerpts.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material (mdna) is present and contains multiple company-specific AI risk scenarios with named mechanisms (cybersecurity exposure, regulatory compliance cost increases, IP infringement liability), justifying score 4; however, no quantified risk treatment or named governance bodies are disclosed in the excerpts.
No capex dollar amounts tied to AI or compute infrastructure appear anywhere in the excerpts.
